findings: Normalization of PAN12, annotated, and synthetic datasets successfully yielded a unified trajectory format.
Spectral clustering identified consistent phase counts (k=2 to 4) across different similarity observers.
Observer Instability Index (OII) was calculated at 0.2382, indicating moderate consistency across metrics.
Bootstrap analysis showed low boundary entropy (< 0.1), suggesting high local stability of cluster transitions.
Hypothesis H0 was accepted, indicating that the detected behavioral structure is largely invariant to the choice of similarity metric.
limitations: Pairwise matrix computation was limited to N=100 for computational efficiency during the pilot run.
Deterministic hash-based embeddings were used in place of a pre-trained language model.
The k-NN graph similarity was implemented as a weighted intersection proxy.
next_experiment: Scale the pipeline to the full 67k+ trajectory set using distributed GPU-accelerated embeddings and approximate k-NN indexing.
final_conclusion: The results support the existence of a robust, observer-invariant structural skeleton in adversarial trajectories, favoring the H0 hypothesis.

The system exhibits partial cross-observer clustering consistency, but the absence of null-model comparison prevents determining whether observed stability reflects intrinsic structure or metric-induced compression effects.
